mnemonics: SIMILAR: Similar Instances Make Intelligent Learning And Reasoning
keyPoints: Learning is based on comparing new instances to stored examples using a similarity or distance metric.
It is an instance-based or lazy learning approach, where generalizing from data is deferred until a query is made.
Common algorithms include k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) and its variants, which classify based on the majority class of the 'k' most similar training examples.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to normalize or standardize features, which can cause features with larger scales to disproportionately influence the distance metric.
Choosing an inappropriate value for 'k' (e.g., k=1 is highly sensitive to noise, while a very large k can smooth over important patterns).
Using the algorithm on very high-dimensional data without dimensionality reduction, leading to the 'curse of dimensionality' where distance metrics become less meaningful.
